column13:
-Extensive beaver activity along visible reach from road to gauge and downstream of gauge.
-jam/dam/logs just down from gauge overrun by water but looks like it is still in tact.
column14: -FT2 wade ~5m upstream of gauge. -MGH taken from DWL value. Trace appears flat with minimal variations. -abrupt changes at edges are accurate. big step down. -chose the spot with a small eddy as it was shallower than upstream and had what looked to be a nice straight velocity mid channel. -@ Uncertainty: IVE method, 2-sigma value (2 x Uncertainty Value reported in *.ft File). @ -Cut taken at small opening below gauge. Usual Spot.  -second rod used for cuts pre and post reset. No change observed on the rod. Mid-section

column12: Measurement condition : Altered; Cloudy , No Precipitation , Very Light Wind Blowing Downstream
column13: Appears similar to previous visit.  Can definitely see composition of beaver dam is mostly small rocks with some sticks piled on top, took photos.  Given the control compostion it is likely quite stable at this point and will likely remain unchanged unless more debris gets lodged on top of existing debris or washed away in the spring.
column14: Measured 10m upstream of gauge.  Considered a decent but not a great section due to the angular flow.  Farther upstream and downstream large negative flows on both edges so wanted to avoid those sections.  Note to RFT do not apply shift as measurements plots below the current rating.  This will require a more in depth review by computing tech. -RESET AT 10:17, offset 7.405  Mid-section

column12: Measurement condition : Altered; Mostly Cloudy , No Precipitation , Light Wind Blowing Downstream
column13: Beaver dam approx 15m downstream of gauge has clearly been blown out.  Able to easily measure at gauge now which wasn't the case previously due to increased depths from beaver dam.
column14: Measured with flow tracker2 about 4m usptream of gauge.  Did not measure right at gauge as there were eddies on both sides.  More panels than expected, will adjust for next visit.  Some roots and branches near the edges but nothing significant.  Could not see them when picking section as water murky with sediment. -Cut taken below gauge at bottom of path to water  -Reset at 17:33. New offset 7.401 Mid-section

column12: Measurement condition : Altered; Cloudy , No Precipitation , Very Light Wind Blowing Downstream
column13: -Looks the same above gauge as visit last month.  Appears the dam/debris would or will flush out in the spring.  Large fallen tree across seems to be holding most of the debris at this time.  Does not appear to be any fresh beaver activity.
column14: Measured right above gauge.  Previous mmnt location change by spring flush out of beaver dam.  No good spots to measure as previous good spot altered and debris in channel at this time making eddies in the channel.  Current mmnt location only OK due to angluar flows and velocity distribution in channel. -Cut taken at usual spot in line with path past gauge to water. Can see what looks like the old shore line from before the water rose due to the beaver dam. Submerged only a few centimeters. Mid-section

-Lots of mud now exposed in front of gauge.
column14: -wade ~5m upstream of gauge with FT2.
